name: Tender Flaky Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 1/2 stick cold margarine (not spread), cut into small cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Prepare a baking sheet by lightly greasing it with shortening or lining it with parchment paper.
3. In a medium bowl, mix together the cake flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
4. Add the cold margarine cubes to the flour mixture. Work them in using pastry cutters or fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs with no larger than small peas.
5. Stir in the buttermilk until the dough comes together as a ball.
6. Place the dough on a lightly floured surface and gently roll it out into a rectangle measuring approximately 10x6 inches.
7. Fold both short ends of the dough so they meet in the center, then fold the dough in half to form a packet with about four layers.
8. Roll the dough into a square measuring about 6 inches on each side.
9. Using a floured knife or pastry wheel, cut the dough into 16 squares by slicing straight down through the center of each piece (avoid sawing motion).
10. Transfer the biscuits to the prepared baking sheet, spacing them about 1-1/2 inches apart.
11. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until the biscuits are lightly browned and cooked through.
12. Remove from the oven and transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.
